grounding_note: Expansion batch: more program-identity and agency-level disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: They're taking money out of my monthly check to repay something from years ago and I barely get by as is.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > Welfare
scenarios.hidden_fact: The deduction is DHS recouping an old TANF overpayment out of my current food benefits.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's DHS clawing back an old TANF overpayment from my current food benefits.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which agency is recouping which benefit
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Social Security is withholding 10% of my SSI for an overpayment from when my mom's babysitting money counted against me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Social Security's withholding a tenth of my SSI over an old overpayment.
scenarios.vetting_note: Recoupment mechanics look identical across agencies; the deducting agency routes.

scenarios.opening_query: The state licensing board sent me a letter and I'm terrified to even open the follow-up.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Professional Licensing || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The letter says they referred my case to the DA for practicing during my lapsed renewal — there's now an actual criminal citation attached.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They referred me to the DA for practicing while my renewal lapsed — there's a criminal citation now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a criminal referral accompanies the board action
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a proposed 90-day suspension with a hearing date — administrative only, no DA involvement.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a proposed 90-day suspension with a hearing — no DA involved.
scenarios.vetting_note: Board-to-DA referral converts an administrative defense into criminal defense.

scenarios.opening_query: My special-needs son is about to turn 18 and I'm told everything about his support changes.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Wills & Trusts > Conservatorship/Guardianship
scenarios.hidden_fact: The urgent piece is decision-making authority — he can't manage medical or financial choices, and the clinic says at 18 I lose authority without a guardianship order.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The urgent part is authority — he can't manage medical or money decisions, and at 18 I lose standing without guardianship.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether adult guardianship is the transition need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The issue is his benefits — at 18 SSI counts only his own income, and we need the adult redetermination handled right.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the benefits — his adult SSI redetermination has to be handled right.
scenarios.vetting_note: Turning-18 transitions split guardianship and benefits routes; families face both.

scenarios.opening_query: I drive for the county and got written up over my CDL medical card situation. My union rep is useless.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Public Employees - Employee || Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The real problem is the DMV — they refuse to process my renewed medical certificate and downgraded my CDL by mistake, which triggered the write-up.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The DMV's the real problem — they won't process my renewed medical cert and wrongly downgraded my CDL, which caused the write-up.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the agency's license processing error is the root cause
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The DMV side is fine now — the county is still pushing discipline over the lapse, and my civil-service appeal window is open.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The DMV part's fixed — the county's still pushing discipline and my civil-service appeal window is open.
scenarios.vetting_note: Root-cause-vs-consequence disclosure; two agencies, two routes.

scenarios.opening_query: Our daughter's college is threatening to withhold her degree over a conduct case that was a joke.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Student Rights || Debtor/Creditor > Student Loans
scenarios.hidden_fact: The conduct case actually closed in her favor — the real hold is a disputed $9,000 'institutional balance' they've sent to collections, and her transcripts are hostage to it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The conduct case closed in her favor — the real hold is a disputed $9,000 balance they sent to collections, with her transcript hostage.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a disputed institutional debt rather than discipline is blocking the degree
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no money owed — it's purely the conduct finding, issued without letting her present witnesses, and we want the process challenged.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No money's owed — it's the conduct finding, made without her witnesses, and we want that process challenged.
scenarios.vetting_note: Transcript-hostage debt vs due-process discipline; the closed-case fact reroutes.

scenarios.opening_query: The pharmacy board flagged my dispensing records and suspended me while they 'investigate'.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Professional Licensing || Criminal Law > White Collar Crime
scenarios.hidden_fact: Federal agents served a subpoena on the pharmacy the same week — my lawyer friend says the parallel criminal fraud investigation is the bigger danger.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Federal agents subpoenaed the pharmacy the same week — the parallel criminal fraud investigation is the real danger.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a parallel criminal investigation exists
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No criminal anything — it's a records-audit dispute over documentation standards, and I need the suspension lifted.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing criminal — it's a records-audit dispute and I need the suspension lifted.
scenarios.vetting_note: Parallel-proceedings fact changes defense priority entirely.

scenarios.opening_query: My benefits hearing is scheduled and everyone tells me not to go in without representation.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Unemployment || Administrative Law > Medicare/Medicaid || Administrative Law > Welfare
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my Medicaid home-care hours — the state cut them from 40 to 12 a week and the hearing is my appeal of that reduction.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my Medicaid home-care hours — cut from 40 to 12, and the hearing is my appeal.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my unemployment appeal — my old employer is contesting my benefits claiming I quit voluntarily.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's unemployment — my old employer claims I quit voluntarily.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Benefits hearing' is maximally ambiguous; program identity is one question away.

scenarios.opening_query: The city shut off our water over a bill from the previous owner of our house.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Utilities || Real Property > General (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The title company missed the recorded utility lien at closing — this is now a title-insurance and escrow-failure claim, not just a shutoff fight.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The title company missed a recorded utility lien at closing — it's a title-insurance and escrow failure now, beyond the shutoff.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a closing/title failure underlies the utility claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No lien was recorded — the city just applied his unpaid balance to our new account, which their own rules don't allow.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No lien exists — the city just moved his old balance onto our account, against their own rules.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same shutoff, but a missed-lien fact reroutes to real-property/title practice.
